title: Jumping Frog: Two Channels, One Belief

runtime.expected_outcome: Observation combines goal_cue + tile + wall_hit channels into one obs index; posterior concentrates faster than any single channel alone.

math.latex: P(o_1, o_2, \ldots \mid s) = \prod_k P(o_k \mid s),\quad q(s \mid o_{1:K}) \propto q(s) \prod_k P(o_k \mid s)

symbols.factorised likelihood: independence of channels given the state

audiences.kid: You see and hear at the same time. Both senses help you decide what's in front of you, faster than either alone.
audiences.real: In the frog_pond world the bundle fuses three channels into one obs index via ObsAdapter. The joint posterior is tighter than any single-channel slice.
audiences.equation: P(o_1:K | s) factorises over channels; log posterior adds log-likelihoods across channels. Multi-modal is multiplicative.
audiences.derivation: Under conditional independence of channels given state, log posterior = log prior + sum_k log P(o_k | s). This is the factor-graph message-passing (Bishop 2006, Ch 8).

orchestrate.objective: Show multi-channel observation fusion on the frog_pond world.
orchestrate.role: Multi-modal tutor using the canonical Jumping Frog pattern.
orchestrate.context: frog_pond 5x3; blanket subscribes goal_cue + tile + wall_hit.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 3.
Bishop (2006), Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Ch 8.
